# hcloudgroup_server_group (Resource)

A group of Hetzner Cloud servers managed as a single rolling-replace unit.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `image` (String) Hetzner image name or numeric id (passed verbatim).
- `location` (String) Hetzner location, e.g. fsn1, nbg1, hel1.
- `name` (String) Group identifier. Embedded in server names and used as a label selector value.
- `network_id` (Number) ID of the hcloud network to attach each server to.
- `replicas` (Number) Number of slots in the group. Must be 1..999. The upper bound pairs with the deterministic server-name budget (group-slot-generation must fit RFC 1123's 63-char limit; see internal/servergroup/validators.go). (Named 'replicas' rather than 'count' because Terraform reserves 'count' as a meta-argument on every resource.)
- `server_type` (String) Hetzner server type, e.g. cx22.

### Optional

- `before_create` (Block, Optional) Pre-create lifecycle hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--before_create))
- `before_remove` (Block, Optional) Pre-remove lifecycle hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--before_remove))
- `before_replace` (Block, Optional) Pre-replace cluster-level hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--before_replace))
- `labels` (Map of String) User labels applied to each server. Must not use the hcloudgroup.io/ prefix.
- `post_create` (Block, Optional) Post-create lifecycle hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--post_create))
- `post_remove` (Block, Optional) Post-remove lifecycle hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--post_remove))
- `post_replace` (Block, Optional) Post-replace cluster-level hook. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--post_replace))
- `readiness_probe` (Block, Optional) Polled command that determines when a freshly-created server is ready. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--readiness_probe))
- `replace_method` (String) How a slot replace orders create vs delete. "create_before_destroy" (default): bring up the new server, run readiness, then delete the old. "destroy_before_create": delete the old first, then create. Note: the default switched in v0.2.0; v0.1.x behaved as if pinned to destroy-first. Pin destroy-first for fixed-membership quorum systems (etcd, consul, RabbitMQ) and when hcloud vCPU quota is tight (create-first transiently uses replicas+1 servers).
- `replace_on_change` (Set of String) Names of additional attributes that, when changed, trigger a rolling replace.
- `ssh_keys` (List of String) Names of pre-existing hcloud SSH keys to authorize on each server.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `user_data_template` (String) Go text/template source for cloud-init user_data. See package docs for variables.

### Read-Only

- `current_replace_hash` (String) SHA-256 of the current desired hash inputs. For drift inspection.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `slots` (Attributes List) One entry per slot, populated after CRUD operations. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--slots))

<a id="nestedblock--before_create"></a>
### Nested Schema for `before_create`

Optional:

- `command` (Block, Optional) Shell command run via /bin/sh -c with a clean environment. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--before_create--command))

<a id="nestedblock--before_create--command"></a>
### Nested Schema for `before_create.command`

Optional:

- `command` (String) Shell command passed to /bin/sh -c. No template interpolation; dynamic values flow via env.
- `env` (Map of String) Full environment for the command. Keys must not start with HCLOUDGROUP_.
- `expected_exit` (Set of Number) Exit codes that count as success. Defaults to {0}.
- `stdin` (String, Sensitive) Bytes fed to the command's stdin. Marked sensitive — operators commonly pipe secrets via stdin.
- `timeout` (String) Per-attempt timeout, e.g. 30s, 5m. Parsed by Go time.ParseDuration. Required when the block is set.
- `working_dir` (String) Working directory. Defaults to a per-action ephemeral tempdir.

- `expected_exit` (Set of Number) Exit codes that count as success. Defaults to {0}.

<a id="nestedblock--readiness_probe"></a>
### Nested Schema for `readiness_probe`

Optional:

- `command` (Block, Optional) Probe command and its polling configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--readiness_probe--command))

<a id="nestedblock--readiness_probe--command"></a>
### Nested Schema for `readiness_probe.command`

Optional:

- `command` (String) Probe command. Same execution semantics as action commands. Required when set.
- `env` (Map of String)
- `expected_exit` (Set of Number)
- `interval` (String) Wait between attempts. Required when set.
- `stdin` (String, Sensitive) Bytes fed to the command's stdin. Marked sensitive — operators commonly pipe secrets via stdin.
- `success_threshold` (Number) Consecutive successful attempts required. Defaults to 1.
- `timeout` (String) Per-attempt timeout. Required when set.
- `total_timeout` (String) Overall deadline; if exceeded, the probe fails. Required when set.
- `working_dir` (String)



<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) Default 60m. Tune up for larger groups or longer probes.
- `delete` (String) Default 30m.
- `update` (String) Default 90m.


<a id="nestedatt--slots"></a>
### Nested Schema for `slots`

Read-Only:

- `generation` (Number)
- `ip_private` (String)
- `last_error` (String)
- `replace_hash` (String)
- `server_id` (Number)
- `server_name` (String)
- `slot_id` (Number)
- `status` (String)
